title: Role-Based Authorization
description: Restrict access based on user roles (admin, user).

slides.title: Adding Roles to Tokens/Sessions
slides.content:
# Roles

A **role** is a category of users with specific permissions. Common roles: `admin`, `user`, `moderator`, `guest`.

In JWT, include role in payload:
```javascript
const token = jwt.sign({ userId, role: user.role }, secret);
```

In sessions, store role in `req.session`.

Then you can create middleware that checks the role.

slides.title: Authorization Middleware by Role
Resource-Based Authorization

quizData.question: What HTTP status code is typically returned when an authenticated user lacks permission?
quizData.explanation: 403 Forbidden indicates the server understood the request but refuses to authorize it.
